answer:
**I could not find a couplet in Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq's *Diwan-E-Zauq* that matches this description.** 

The corpus contains a complete translation of the *Diwan* (60 chapters/ghazals). I scanned the work-level wiki card, read chapter wiki cards for candidates (e.g., ch. 15, 28, 39, 52, 56, 58, 59 mentioning fire, heart, sighs, smoke, or complaints/"shikayat"), performed multiple *search_corpus* queries for English terms ("breath", "flame"/"fire"/"blaze"/"burning", "heart", "complaint", "sigh") and Urdu concepts ("shikayat"/"shikwa"/"naala"/"nafas"/"dam"/"aah"), and fully read several chapters (28, 39, 59) plus their transliterations.

Relevant near-misses:
- Ch. 39 opens: "If I had not suppressed my sigh (*naala*) of pain, / A smoke would rise..."[^1] (sigh suppressed to prevent smoke, implying inner fire—but no breath or explicit heart-flame comparison).
- Ch. 28 opens: "Khoob roka shikayaton se mujhe" ("You well stopped my complaints (*shikayaton*)")—complaints silenced, but no breath/flame/heart.[^2]
- Ch. 58: "My heart's fire (*dil ka aag*) will die..."—heart-fire, but no breath/complaint.[^3]
- Ch. 59: "aur jo yeh tang karein munh se shikayat na kare" ("when they distress, offer no complaint (*shikayat*) from the mouth")—verbal complaint advised against, no breath/flame/heart.[^4]

No couplet compares a "breath (*nafas*/*dam*) at the moment of complaint" directly to "a flame (*shola*) on the heart (*dil*)". It may be from an uncorpus-ed section, variant reading, or misattributed. 

[^1]: *Diwan-E-Zauq*, ch. 39, [p-1b63cf](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/39-na-karta-zabt-main-naala-to-phir-aisa-dhuaan-hota/translation/#p-1b63cf).

[^2]: *Diwan-E-Zauq*, ch. 28, [p-fb1017](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/28-khoob-roka-shikayaton-se-mujhe/translation/#p-fb1017).

[^3]: *Diwan-E-Zauq*, ch. 58 wiki card (key passage).

[^4]: *Diwan-E-Zauq*, ch. 59, [p-d2f568](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/59-koi-in-tang-dahanon-se-mohabbat-na-kare/translation/#p-d2f568).
